# mem0-mcp-go

> Go-native MCP server for self-hosted Mem0 OSS. Provides memory add,
> search, get, update, delete, history, and doctor tools over stdio or
> SSE transport.

## Overview

- Name: mem0-mcp-go
- Purpose: Go-native MCP server bridging self-hosted Mem0 OSS to MCP clients (Cursor, Claude Code); provides 7 memory management tools with optional dual-write fan-out to cloud and backup targets.
- Language(s): Go
- License: MIT
- Authoritative README: <https://github.com/nfsarch33/mem0-mcp-go#readme>
- Source of truth branch: `main`

## Quick Start

- Build and run:
  ```
  go run ./cmd/mem0-mcp-go --version
  export MEM0_BASE_URL="http://<your-mem0-host>:<port>"
  ```
- MCP client config:
  ```json
  {
    "mcpServers": {
      "mem0": {
        "command": "mem0-mcp-go",
        "env": {
          "MEM0_BASE_URL": "http://<your-mem0-host>:<port>"
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```
- Run tests: `go test ./...`
- Minimum runtime prerequisites: Go 1.22+, self-hosted Mem0 instance

## Key Concepts

- Tools: `mem0_add`, `mem0_search`, `mem0_get_all`, `mem0_update`, `mem0_delete`, `mem0_history`, `mem0_doctor`
- Configuration via environment variables: `MEM0_BASE_URL` (required), `MEM0_API_KEY`, `MEM0_USER_ID`, `MEM0_APP_ID`, `MCP_TRANSPORT`, `LOG_LEVEL`
- Dual-write support: fan out writes to cloud (`MEM0_CLOUD_URL`) and backup (`MEM0_BACKUP_URL`) while keeping OSS as primary
- Read source configurable: `MEM0_READ_SOURCE` (`oss` or `cloud`)
- Transport: `stdio` (default) or `sse` (`:9092`)
- Do not pass secrets on argv; use environment variables or 1Password

## Runnable Examples

- Build: `go build ./cmd/mem0-mcp-go`
- Run tests: `go test ./...`

## Operational Notes

- Issue tracker: <https://github.com/nfsarch33/mem0-mcp-go/issues>
